Filling: 300g boneless chicken (cooked & shredded), 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp paprika, 1 tbsp minced garlic, ½ tsp black pepper, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 2–3 diced green chilies, salt, 2 tbsp oil, mixed veggies (capsicum, onion—optional), 2 tbsp flour, ½ cup milk, ½ cup shredded cheese, chili garlic sauce/ketchup, egg wash, sesame/black seeds
directions: Step 1: Activate yeast in warm milk + sugar 5–10 mins. Step 2: Mix flour, salt, egg, butter; add yeast mix; knead soft dough (8–10 mins). Rise 1–2 hrs until double. Step 3: Cook chicken with spices/oil; add veggies/flour/milk; thicken. Cool; add cheese/sauce. Step 4: Divide dough; roll rectangle; spread filling; roll tightly; shape loaf. Step 5: Egg wash; sprinkle seeds. Bake 180°C 25–30 mins golden. Step 6: Cool slightly; slice. Tip: Don’t overfill—leaks.
nutrition: 450–500 Calories per slice, Protein 18–22g
health_recommendation: Chicken protein; bread carbs. Moderate fat—use less cheese/oil. Hearty snack/breakfast.

ingredients: 500g boneless chicken breast (cut strips), 1 tsp baking powder (for crisp), 2 tbsp mayonnaise, 2 tbsp chipotle sauce, 1 tbsp ketchup, 1 tbsp honey, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp red chili powder, salt, ½ cup flour, ¼ cup cornflour, 1 tsp paprika, ½ tsp black pepper, 2 eggs beaten, oil for frying
directions: Step 1: Marinate chicken with mayonnaise, chipotle, ketchup, honey, garlic powder, soy, chili powder, salt 30 mins. Step 2: Mix flour, cornflour, paprika, black pepper, baking powder. Step 3: Dip chicken in egg, then flour mix (press for coating). Step 4: Fry in hot oil 4–5 mins until golden-crisp. Step 5: Toss in extra sauce if desired. Serve hot. Tip: Baking powder for extra crisp.
nutrition: 420–480 Calories, 28–32g Fat, Protein 30–35g
health_recommendation: Chicken protein; fried—high fat/calories; air-fry healthier. Spicy appetizer—occasional.

ingredients: Filling: 300g boneless chicken (minced/cooked), ½ cup chicken stock, 1 tbsp chili sauce, 1 tbsp vinegar, ½ tsp black pepper, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp dark soy sauce, ½ tsp chili flakes, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tbsp minced garlic, 1 cup shredded cabbage, ½ cup capsicum, ½ cup carrot, ½ cup cooked noodles, 2 tbsp green onions, ½ tsp sugar. Wrapper: 6–8 egg roll wrappers (or make with flour), oil for frying
directions: Step 1: Heat oil; sauté garlic. Step 2: Add chicken; cook until done. Step 3: Add stock, sauces, spices, sugar; simmer. Step 4: Add veggies/noodles/green onions; cook 3–4 mins (dry filling). Step 5: Cool filling. Step 6: Place filling on wrapper; roll tightly (seal edges with water/flour paste). Step 7: Fry in hot oil until golden-crisp. Serve with sauce. Tip: Don’t overfill—seals properly.
nutrition: 300–350 Calories per roll, Protein 15–18g
health_recommendation: Chicken protein; veggies fiber. Fried—air-fry healthier. Tasty snack.

ingredients: ½ cup whole wheat/broken wheat, ¼ cup barley, ¼ cup red lentil, ¼ cup yellow lentil, ¼ cup Bengal gram, ¼ cup rice (all soaked overnight), 500g boneless chicken, ½ cup yogurt, 2 tbsp green chili paste, 2 tbsp ginger paste, 2 tbsp garlic paste, 2 tbsp haleem spice mix, ½ tsp turmeric, salt, ½ cup oil, 2 onions sliced, chicken stock, garnish: fried onions, chopped garlic, cumin seeds, mint, ginger, green chilies, chat masala
directions: Step 1: Soak all grains overnight; drain. Step 2: Pressure cook grains with chicken, spices, yogurt, pastes, turmeric, salt, oil 4–5 whistles. Step 3: Shred chicken; mash everything to thick consistency (add stock if needed). Step 4: Fry onions golden; temper with cumin, garlic. Step 5: Add to haleem; simmer 30 mins. Step 6: Garnish & serve hot. Tip: Slow cook for authentic texture.
nutrition: 450–500 Calories, 20–25g Fat, Protein 30g
health_recommendation: Protein from chicken/dal; grains fiber. Rich—occasional; use less oil. Nourishing one-pot meal.

ingredients: 500g chicken (boneless or bone-in thighs for juiciness), 2 tbsp cornflour (for coating & thickening), ½ tsp black pepper powder, 1 tsp salt, 3–4 tbsp oil, 1 medium onion finely sliced, 1 tbsp green chili paste, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 2 medium tomatoes pureed, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp turmeric powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder (for color), ½ tsp red chili powder (adjust heat), ½ cup yogurt (whisked), 2 tbsp butter, 1 tsp dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi—crushed), ¼ cup fresh cream (for richness), ginger juliennes & coriander for garnish
directions: Step 1: Marinate chicken with 1 tbsp cornflour, ½ tsp black pepper, ½ tsp salt, 1 tbsp oil for 30 mins (helps tenderize & crisp). Step 2: Heat 2–3 tbsp oil in heavy-bottom handi/kadai on medium. Add sliced onion; fry 6–8 mins until golden-brown (stir often—golden onions give sweetness). Step 3: Add ginger-garlic paste & green chili paste; sauté 1–2 mins until raw smell disappears. Step 4: Add tomato puree, coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric, Kashmiri chili powder, red chili powder, remaining salt; cook 8–10 mins on medium until oil separates & masala thickens. Step 5: Add marinated chicken; stir-fry on high 5–7 mins until color changes & oil separates. Step 6: Whisk yogurt; add gradually while stirring (prevents curdling). Cook 5 mins. Step 7: Add ½–¾ cup water (for gravy); cover & simmer on low 15–20 mins until chicken tender & oil floats. Step 8: Add butter, kasuri methi (crush between palms), garam masala, cream; simmer 3–5 mins until glossy & thick. Garnish ginger juliennes & coriander. Serve hot with naan/roti. Tip: Low heat after yogurt prevents splitting; don’t overcook chicken.
nutrition: 420–460 Calories, 28–32g Fat, Protein 32–35g, Sodium 900–1100mg
health_recommendation: High protein from chicken (muscle repair/satiety); yogurt probiotics for gut health. Spices (turmeric, cumin) anti-inflammatory/digestion aid. Moderate oil/butter—healthier by using 2 tbsp total & low-fat yogurt. Creamy Pakistani curry—great family meal; pair with salad/veggies for fiber.

ingredients: 1 kg skinless chicken pieces (bone-in thighs/legs for flavor), ½ cup oil/ghee, 4 medium onions thinly sliced (for rich base), ¼ cup cashews (soaked & ground to paste for creaminess), 1 cup yogurt (whisked), 4–5 cloves, 4 green cardamoms, 1 tsp cumin seeds, 8–10 black peppercorns, 1-inch cinnamon stick, 2 black cardamoms, 1 bay leaf, 1 tbsp green chili paste, 1 tbsp garlic paste, 1 tbsp ginger paste, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder, ½ tsp turmeric powder, salt to taste, 1 tsp kewra water (for aroma—optional), 2–3 cups water
directions: Step 1: Marinate chicken with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, green chili paste, all dry spices (coriander, red chili, Kashmiri chili, turmeric), salt for 1 hr (or overnight). Step 2: Heat ½ cup oil/ghee in heavy pot; add whole spices (cloves, green/black cardamoms, cumin seeds, peppercorns, cinnamon, bay leaf); crackle 30–40 secs. Step 3: Add sliced onions; fry 12–15 mins until deep golden-brown (stir often—golden onions give sweetness & color). Step 4: Add marinated chicken; stir-fry on high 8–10 mins until color changes & oil separates. Step 5: Add cashew paste; stir 2–3 mins. Step 6: Add 2–3 cups water; bring to boil, then cover & simmer on low 35–45 mins until chicken tender & gravy thickens (oil floats on top). Step 7: Add kewra water (if using); simmer 2 mins. Adjust salt. Serve hot with naan/rice. Tip: Slow simmer for creamy texture; don’t add water after cashew paste.
nutrition: 480–550 Calories, 35–40g Fat, Protein 35–40g, Sodium 900–1100mg
health_recommendation: Chicken protein; yogurt probiotics; cashews healthy fats. Rich gravy—occasional; use less oil/ghee, low-fat yogurt. Royal Pakistani dish—pair with salad.

ingredients: 500g boneless chicken (minced or finely chopped), 2 tbsp cornflour, 1 egg (beaten—for binding), ½ tsp black pepper powder, salt to taste, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp red chili crushed/flakes, 4–5 garlic cloves minced, 1 small capsicum diced, 1 medium onion diced, 2 tbsp tomato ketchup, ½ cup chicken stock, 1 tbsp tomato paste, 1 tbsp honey, 1 tbsp soy sauce (extra), salt/pepper, oil for frying, instant ramen (crushed for crunch—optional), green onions chopped, lettuce leaves (for wrapping)
directions: Step 1: Mix chicken with cornflour, egg, black pepper, salt, 1 tbsp soy sauce; marinate 30 mins. Step 2: Heat oil; fry chicken on high 6–8 mins until golden/crisp (stir often). Remove; set aside. Step 3: In same pan, add 1 tbsp oil; sauté garlic & red chili flakes 30 secs. Step 4: Add onion & capsicum; stir-fry 2–3 mins (crisp-tender). Step 5: Add ketchup, tomato paste, honey, soy sauce, chicken stock; boil & thicken 2–3 mins. Step 6: Add fried chicken; toss 2 mins. Add green onions. Step 7: Serve in lettuce leaves; top with crushed ramen for crunch. Tip: High heat for crisp chicken.
nutrition: 320–360 Calories, 18–22g Fat, Protein 28–32g
health_recommendation: Chicken protein; veggies fiber. Lettuce low-carb wrap. Moderate oil—air-fry chicken healthier. Spicy, light appetizer.

ingredients: 1 kg chicken (bone-in pieces), ½ cup oil, 2 medium onions sliced, 2–3 tbsp nihari masala (store-bought or homemade), 2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tbsp green chili paste, ½ cup wheat flour slurry (for thickening), ½ cup plain yogurt, 2 tbsp ghee, 1 tsp red chili powder, ginger juliennes, fresh coriander, lemon wedges, salt to taste, 4–5 cups water
directions: Step 1: Heat ½ cup oil; fry onions golden (10–12 mins). Step 2: Add ginger-garlic paste, green chili paste; sauté 1–2 mins. Step 3: Add chicken; fry 8–10 mins. Step 4: Add nihari masala, red chili powder, salt; stir 2 mins. Step 5: Add yogurt; cook 5 mins until oil separates. Step 6: Add water; pressure cook 4–5 whistles (or simmer 2–3 hrs) until tender. Step 7: Mix wheat flour with water; add slurry; simmer 10–15 mins to thicken. Step 8: Heat 2 tbsp ghee; temper with chili powder; pour over. Garnish ginger/coriander/lemon. Serve hot with naan. Tip: Slow cook for rich flavor.
nutrition: 450–500 Calories, 30–35g Fat, Protein 35–40g
health_recommendation: Protein from chicken; wheat fiber. Rich—occasional; use less oil. Traditional slow-cooked comfort.

recipe_name: Homemade Chicken Nuggets with Ground Chicken
ingredients: 4 slices bread (soaked in ½ cup milk for binding), 500g ground chicken (minced breast/thigh), 1 tsp onion powder, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tsp chicken powder (optional), 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp black pepper, 2 eggs (1 for mix, 1 for coating), 1 cup bread crumbs, oil for frying
directions: Step 1: Soak bread in milk 5 mins; squeeze dry; crumble. Step 2: Mix ground chicken, crumbled bread, onion powder, garlic powder, chicken powder, lemon juice, salt, pepper, 1 egg. Knead well. Step 3: Chill mixture 30 mins (easier shaping). Step 4: Shape into nuggets (oval/round). Step 5: Beat 1 egg; dip nuggets in egg, then bread crumbs (press for coating). Step 6: Fry in hot oil 4–5 mins until golden-crisp. Drain. Serve hot with sauce. Tip: Bake/air-fry 200°C 15–20 mins for healthier.
nutrition: 280–320 Calories, 18–22g Fat, Protein 25–28g
health_recommendation: Chicken protein; bread carbs. Fried—air-fry healthier. Kid-friendly snack.

ingredients: 500g skinless boneless chicken (cut small cubes), ½ tsp baking powder (for crisp), 1 cup gram flour (besan), ¼ cup rice flour (for extra crisp), 1 medium onion sliced, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp red chili flakes, 1 tsp dried fenugreek leaves (crushed), 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tbsp lemon juice, salt to taste, 2 tbsp chopped coriander, 1 tsp turmeric powder, 1 tsp green chili paste, 2–3 sliced green chilies, water as needed (thick batter), oil for deep frying
directions: Step 1: Marinate chicken with ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, salt, green chili paste 30 mins. Step 2: Mix gram flour, rice flour, baking powder, all dry spices (red chili flakes, fenugreek, cumin, coriander, turmeric), salt, chopped coriander, sliced onion, sliced green chilies. Step 3: Add marinated chicken; mix. Add water gradually for thick coating batter (should coat chicken evenly). Step 4: Heat oil on medium. Drop spoonfuls or coated pieces; fry 4–6 mins until golden-crisp. Step 5: Drain on paper. Serve hot with chutney. Tip: Medium heat for even cooking; don’t overcrowd.
nutrition: 320–360 Calories, 20–24g Fat, Protein 25–28g
health_recommendation: Chicken protein; gram flour fiber/protein. Fried—air-fry healthier. Spicy snack—occasional.

ingredients: Filling: 400g ground chicken, 1 tbsp ginger paste, 1 tbsp garlic paste, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tbsp green chili paste, 2 tbsp oil/butter, 1 tsp red chili flakes, ½ tsp black pepper, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, salt to taste, 2 tbsp chopped coriander, 1 medium onion finely chopped, 2–3 chopped green chilies. Dough: 2 cups flour, ½ tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, water as needed. Oil for deep frying
directions: Step 1: Heat 2 tbsp oil; sauté ginger-garlic paste, green chili paste 1 min. Step 2: Add ground chicken; cook 8–10 mins until browned. Step 3: Add all spices, onion, green chilies, coriander, lemon juice; cook 5–7 mins until dry. Cool completely. Step 4: Knead dough with flour, salt, oil, water (soft, smooth). Rest 30 mins. Step 5: Divide dough; roll thin oval; cut in half; shape cone; fill with chicken mixture; seal edges with water. Step 6: Heat oil on medium-low; fry samosas 6–8 mins until golden-crisp. Drain. Serve hot with chutney. Tip: Seal tightly; low-medium heat for even cooking.
nutrition: 250–300 Calories per samosa, 15–18g Fat, Protein 12–15g
health_recommendation: Chicken protein; dough carbs. Fried—bake/air-fry healthier. Popular snack—occasional.

ingredients: 150g bittersweet chocolate (chopped), ½ cup unsalted butter (113g), ¾ cup powdered white sugar (90g), ½ cup brown sugar (100g, loosely packed—for chewiness), 2 large eggs, ½ cup cocoa powder (50g), ¾ cup all-purpose flour (90g), ½ cup chopped walnuts (60g—for crunch), ¼ tsp salt
directions: Step 1: Preheat oven to 180°C/350°F. Line 8×8 inch pan with parchment. Step 2: Melt chocolate & butter over double boiler or microwave (stir every 30 secs—don’t overheat). Step 3: Whisk in powdered sugar & brown sugar until smooth. Step 4: Add eggs one at a time; whisk vigorously 1 min each. Step 5: Sift cocoa, flour, salt; fold gently into wet mixture (don’t overmix). Step 6: Fold in walnuts. Step 7: Spread in pan; bake 25–30 mins (toothpick moist crumbs). Step 8: Cool completely; cut. Tip: Underbake slightly for fudgy center.
nutrition: 250–280 Calories per piece, 18–20g Fat, Sugar 20–22g
health_recommendation: Walnuts omega-3 (heart/brain); chocolate antioxidants. High sugar/fat—treat; reduce sugar, use dark chocolate. Rich dessert.

ingredients: 4 cups water, 2–3 cinnamon sticks (for warm aroma), 2–3 tbsp honey (adjust sweetness), 4–5 green cardamoms (crushed), 2–3 pieces dried orange peel (or fresh zest), 1-inch grated fresh ginger, 4–5 whole cloves, 1–2 tsp black tea leaves (or green tea), 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1–2 tbsp orange juice (optional)
directions: Step 1: In pot, boil 4 cups water with cinnamon sticks, cardamoms, dried orange peel, grated ginger, cloves. Step 2: Simmer 5–7 mins to infuse flavors. Step 3: Add black tea leaves (or green tea); steep 2–3 mins (don’t oversteep—bitter). Step 4: Strain into cups. Step 5: Stir in honey, lemon juice, orange juice (if using). Step 6: Serve hot. Tip: Adjust spices to taste; add tulsi for variation.
nutrition: 50–80 Calories per cup
health_recommendation: Ginger/cinnamon digestion/anti-inflammatory; honey natural sweetener. Low calorie—healthy warm drink. Great for colds/immunity.

ingredients: 2 cups desiccated coconut (200g—fine for smooth texture), 1 can (400g) sweetened condensed milk, 100g milk chocolate (melted—for coating, optional)
directions: Step 1: In bowl, mix 2 cups desiccated coconut with condensed milk until sticky dough forms (adjust coconut if too wet). Step 2: Grease hands; roll into small balls (1-inch diameter). Step 3: Place on parchment-lined tray; chill 30–60 mins to firm. Step 4: Melt milk chocolate (double boiler or microwave). Step 5: Dip balls in chocolate or drizzle; chill 30 mins to set. Step 6: Store in airtight container (fridge up to 1 week). Tip: Roll in extra coconut for coating.
nutrition: 120–150 Calories per ball, Fat 8–10g
health_recommendation: Coconut healthy fats; condensed milk calcium. High sugar—small portions. Sweet treat—use less condensed milk.

ingredients: 1 cup pitted dates (200g—soft Medjool best), ½ cup almonds (soaked & peeled), ¼ cup desiccated coconut, 2 tbsp cocoa powder (for chocolate flavor), pinch pink salt, 1–2 tbsp butter/ghee (for binding)
directions: Step 1: Blend dates, almonds, cocoa powder, pink salt to sticky mixture (pulse—don’t overblend). Step 2: Add butter/ghee if too dry; mix. Step 3: Roll into balls; coat in coconut. Step 4: Chill 30 mins to firm. Store refrigerated. Tip: No added sugar—dates natural sweet.
nutrition: 80–100 Calories per ball, Fiber 3–4g
health_recommendation: Dates natural fiber/iron; nuts healthy fats. No added sugar—healthy energy balls. Great snack.

ingredients: ½ cup almonds, ½ cup cashews, ½ cup pistachios, ½ cup walnuts, 2 tbsp sesame seeds, 1 cup pitted dates, 1–2 tbsp butter/ghee, ¼ cup raisins, ½ tsp cardamom powder
directions: Step 1: Blend nuts & sesame seeds coarsely. Step 2: Blend dates, butter/ghee, raisins, cardamom to paste. Step 3: Mix nut mix with date paste. Step 4: Roll into log; wrap in cling film. Step 5: Chill 1 hr; slice. Tip: Coat in coconut optional.
nutrition: 150–180 Calories per slice
health_recommendation: Dates fiber/iron; nuts healthy fats/protein. No added sugar—nutritious sweet.

ingredients: 10–12 dried dates (kharik—pitted & chopped), 1 liter milk, 1 cup water, ½ cup milk powder, ½ can condensed milk, ¼ cup powdered nuts (almonds/pistachios), ½ tsp green cardamom powder, crushed almonds/pistachios for garnish
directions: Step 1: Soak dried dates in water overnight; drain & chop. Step 2: Boil milk + water; add dates; simmer 20–25 mins until soft. Step 3: Add milk powder, condensed milk; stir until thick. Step 4: Add cardamom, powdered nuts; cook 5 mins. Step 5: Cool; garnish crushed nuts. Serve chilled/warm. Tip: Creamy Bohra dessert.
nutrition: 350–400 Calories, 15–18g Fat
health_recommendation: Dates fiber/iron; milk calcium/protein. Rich—occasional; reduce condensed milk. Nourishing dessert.

ingredients: 10–12 dried dates (pitted & chopped), 2 cups milk, 2 eggs (beaten), 1 can sweetened condensed milk, ½ cup cream, chocolate syrup (for drizzling), ¼ cup walnuts/almonds chopped
directions: Step 1: Blend dates with milk to smooth. Step 2: Mix eggs, condensed milk, cream, date milk. Step 3: Pour into greased dish; bake 180°C 20–25 mins or steam 30 mins until set. Step 4: Cool; chill 4 hrs. Step 5: Drizzle chocolate syrup; garnish nuts. Tip: Creamy baked pudding.
nutrition: 320–360 Calories, 15–18g Fat
health_recommendation: Dates fiber; milk/eggs protein. Rich—occasional; use low-fat milk. Sweet treat.

ingredients: 200g pack caramel biscuits like Biscoff (crushed for spiced caramel base), 100g butter (melted—for binding base), 300g dulce de leche (store-bought or homemade—for rich caramel sweetness), 500ml whipping cream (chilled—for airy texture), 2 egg whites (for volume—use pasteurized for safety), 10g gelatin crystals (bloomed—for setting), 1 tsp vanilla or caramel essence (for aroma), salted caramel sauce (for drizzling—optional)
directions: Step 1: Crush Biscoff biscuits to fine crumbs (processor or rolling pin). Mix with melted butter until sand-like. Press firmly into 8-inch springform pan or individual glasses. Chill 20 mins. Step 2: Bloom gelatin: Sprinkle 10g gelatin over ¼ cup cold water; let stand 5 mins. Microwave 10–15 secs or heat gently until dissolved (clear liquid). Step 3: Whip 500ml chilled cream to soft peaks (medium speed, 2–3 mins—avoid overwhipping). Step 4: Whip 2 egg whites to stiff peaks (pinch salt helps—3–4 mins). Step 5: Fold dulce de leche, vanilla/caramel essence, and cooled gelatin into whipped cream (spatula—retain air). Fold in egg whites gently for lightness. Step 6: Pour over chilled base. Smooth top; chill 4 hrs or overnight. Step 7: Drizzle salted caramel sauce before serving. Serve chilled. Tip: Fold gently—overmixing deflates mousse.
nutrition: 380–420 Calories, 28–32g Fat (65–75%), Saturated Fat 18–20g (90%), Sugar 35–40g, Protein 5–6g (10%)
health_recommendation: Dulce de leche provides calcium/protein from milk; cream adds richness. Egg whites protein boost. High sugar/fat—occasional treat; healthier by using Greek yogurt instead of some cream (cuts fat 20–30%), reduce dulce de leche. Luxurious no-bake dessert—satisfying for special occasions.

ingredients: 500g ground meat (beef/mutton—20% fat for juiciness), ½ cup yogurt (for tenderness), 1 tbsp green chili paste, 1 tbsp raw papaya paste (natural tenderizer), 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp red chili flakes, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder, ½ tsp black pepper, ½ tsp black cumin (shah jeera), salt to taste, ¼ cup oil + 2 tbsp ghee, 2 medium onions thinly sliced, 2 tbsp gram flour (besan—for binding), piece of charcoal (for dum smoke), ginger juliennes, mint leaves, 1 tbsp butter
directions: Step 1: Marinate ground meat with yogurt, papaya paste, ginger-garlic paste, green chili paste, all dry spices (chili flakes, coriander, cumin, garam masala, red/Kashmiri chili, black pepper, black cumin), salt for 1 hr (papaya tenderizes). Step 2: Heat ¼ cup oil + 2 tbsp ghee; fry sliced onions 10–12 mins until deep golden-brown (stir often). Step 3: Add marinated keema; cook on high 10–15 mins until water evaporates & color changes (stir continuously). Step 4: Sprinkle 2 tbsp gram flour; stir 2–3 mins to roast & bind. Step 5: Cover & cook on low (dum style) 30–40 mins (seal with dough for traditional dum—stir midway; add ¼ cup water if dry). Step 6: Heat charcoal till red; place in small foil bowl with butter on keema; cover 5 mins for smoke. Step 7: Garnish ginger juliennes, mint leaves. Serve hot with naan/paratha. Tip: Low dum heat for soft keema; charcoal smoke optional but authentic.
nutrition: 450–500 Calories, 32–38g Fat, Protein 30–35g, Iron high
health_recommendation: Mutton protein/iron (energy/blood health); yogurt probiotics; papaya enzymes aid digestion. Gram flour fiber/protein. High fat—use lean meat/less ghee. Smoky, tender keema—festive dish; pair with salad.

ingredients: 2 medium eggplants (firm, sliced ½-inch thick rounds/strips), 2 cups water + 1 cup milk (soaking—for tenderness), 1 tsp salt (soaking), 1 cup flour (for batter), 1 tsp chicken powder (or veg seasoning), 1 tsp dry basil leaves, 2 eggs beaten (for coating), salt & pepper to taste, 1½ cups bread crumbs (panko for extra crisp), oil for deep frying
directions: Step 1: Slice eggplant; soak in salted water-milk 15–20 mins (draws bitterness/moisture). Drain & pat dry thoroughly. Step 2: Mix flour, chicken powder, dry basil, salt, pepper with water to thick batter. Step 3: Dip eggplant in batter, then egg, then press into bread crumbs. Step 4: Heat oil (medium-high); fry batches 3–4 mins until golden-crisp. Step 5: Drain; serve hot with ketchup/mayo. Tip: Pat dry well—wet fries soggy. Air-fry 200°C 12–15 mins healthier.
nutrition: 220–260 Calories per serving (5–6 fries), 15–18g Fat, Fiber 4–5g
health_recommendation: Eggplant fiber/antioxidants (heart/digestion); low calorie veggie. Fried—air-fry healthier. Crispy snack—add spices.

ingredients: 1 kg carrots (red variety—grated, 7–8 cups), ½–¾ cup ghee (100–150g—more richer), 1–1½ cups full-fat milk, ¾–1 cup sugar (adjust), ½–¾ cup khoya/mawa grated (for creaminess), ½ tsp cardamom powder, 15–20 cashews/almonds chopped, 1 tbsp raisins, pinch saffron (optional)
directions: Step 1: Grate carrots; squeeze lightly if watery. Step 2: Heat ½ cup ghee; fry nuts/raisins golden; set aside. Step 3: Add carrots; sauté 10–15 mins until soft/water evaporates. Step 4: Add hot milk; simmer 30–40 mins until thick (stir often). Step 5: Add sugar/khoya; cook 15–20 mins until ghee separates/thick. Step 6: Add cardamom/saffron; garnish nuts. Serve warm. Tip: Slow cook authentic.
nutrition: 350 Calories, 18g Fat (46%), Vitamin A very high, Protein 8g (16%)
health_recommendation: Carrots beta-carotene (eye/skin); milk calcium; khoya protein. Rich—occasional; reduce ghee/sugar. Festive nutrient boost.

ingredients: ½ cup ghee (100g—for richness), ½ cup mixed nuts (almonds/cashews/pistachios—chopped), 2 tbsp gond katira (edible gum—crispy texture), ½ cup semolina (suji—for grainy texture), ½ cup wheat flour, ½ tsp cardamom powder, pinch saffron (soaked), 1 cup jaggery (grated—for natural sweetness/iron)
directions: Step 1: Heat ½ cup ghee; fry gond katira until puffed/crisp (remove). Step 2: In same ghee, roast semolina & wheat flour low 8–10 mins golden/nutty (stir constantly). Step 3: Add nuts; roast 2 mins. Step 4: Add jaggery; stir until melts & mixture thickens/leaves sides (5–7 mins). Step 5: Add cardamom/saffron; mix. Add fried gond katira. Step 6: Spread on greased plate; cool slightly; cut diamonds. Tip: Low heat prevents burning.
nutrition: 150–180 Calories per piece, Fat 10–12g
health_recommendation: Nuts healthy fats/protein; jaggery iron. Ghee rich—reduce for lighter. Festive sweet—small portions.

ingredients: 1 kg fresh grapes (seedless or deseeded—sweet variety), 600–800g granulated sugar (adjust to 60–80% grape weight), 3–4 tbsp fresh lemon juice (for pectin/tartness), pinch salt (enhances flavor)
directions: Step 1: Wash grapes; remove stems. Mash lightly. Step 2: In pan, combine grapes, sugar, lemon juice, salt. Let sit 30 mins (releases juices). Step 3: Boil medium; reduce low-medium; simmer 35–45 mins, stirring often (skim foam). Step 4: Mash for smooth or leave chunky. Test: cold plate—wrinkles when pushed. Step 5: Cool; jar. Refrigerate 3–4 weeks. Tip: No pectin—long simmer sets.
nutrition: 50–60 Calories per tbsp, Sugar 10–12g
health_recommendation: Grapes antioxidants/vitamin C; no added pectin—natural. High sugar—use less or jaggery. Healthy homemade jam.

ingredients: 1 cup mint leaves (packed), 1 cup cilantro leaves (packed), ½ cup yogurt (for creaminess), ¼ cup fresh coconut (grated—for sweetness), 2 tbsp lemon juice, 3–4 garlic cloves, 2–3 green chili paste (adjust heat), ½ tsp sugar, 1 tsp roasted cumin seeds (powdered), salt to taste
directions: Step 1: Wash mint/cilantro thoroughly; drain. Step 2: Blend all ingredients to smooth paste (add water 1 tbsp at a time if thick). Step 3: Taste; adjust salt/sugar/lemon. Step 4: Store refrigerated 4–5 days. Tip: Fresh herbs key—don’t overblend (loses color).
nutrition: 30–40 Calories per 2 tbsp, Protein 2g
health_recommendation: Mint/cilantro digestion; yogurt probiotics. Low calorie—healthy dip. Add more yogurt for raita.

ingredients: 1 kg goat trotters (paya—cleaned), ½ cup oil/ghee, 2 medium onions sliced, 1 tbsp green chili paste, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp coriander powder, 4–5 cloves, 8–10 peppercorns, 4–5 cups water, 2–3 tbsp wheat flour slurry (thickening), fresh coriander, freshly ground black pepper, salt to taste
directions: Step 1: Clean trotters thoroughly. Step 2: Heat oil/ghee; fry onions golden (10–12 mins). Step 3: Add ginger-garlic paste, green chili paste; sauté 1 min. Step 4: Add trotters, spices, salt; fry 8–10 mins. Step 5: Add water; pressure cook 5–6 whistles (or simmer 3–4 hrs) until gelatinous. Step 6: Add flour slurry; simmer 10 mins to thicken. Step 7: Garnish coriander/black pepper. Serve hot. Tip: Slow cook for rich broth.
nutrition: 350–400 Calories, 25–30g Fat, Protein 30–35g, Collagen high
health_recommendation: Collagen from bones (joints/skin); protein/iron. Rich—occasional; use less oil. Nourishing winter soup.

ingredients: 1 kg guavas (ripe—peeled/seeded/chopped), ½–¾ cup water, 600–800g sugar (adjust), 3–4 tbsp lemon juice, 1 cinnamon stick, 2–3 cardamoms, pinch saffron (optional)
directions: Step 1: Peel/seed/chop guavas; cook with water 15–20 mins until soft. Step 2: Mash or blend smooth. Step 3: Add sugar, lemon juice, cinnamon, cardamoms, saffron; simmer 25–30 mins until thick (coats spoon). Step 4: Test on cold plate. Cool; jar. Refrigerate 3–4 weeks. Tip: Low sugar for tart jam.
nutrition: 50–60 Calories per tbsp, Sugar 10–12g
health_recommendation: Guava vitamin C/fiber; no pectin—natural. High sugar—reduce. Healthy homemade jam.

ingredients: ½ cup whole wheat kernels, ¼ cup barley, ¼ cup rice, ¼ cup red lentil, ¼ cup yellow lentil, ¼ cup Bengal gram (soaked overnight), 1 tbsp green chili paste, 1 tbsp ginger paste, 1 tbsp garlic paste, salt, ½ cup oil, 2 large onions sliced, 2–3 tbsp haleem masala, 500g boneless beef, 6–8 cups water, garnish: oil, chopped ginger/garlic, cumin seeds, round button chilies, fried onions, mint, chat masala, green chilies
directions: Step 1: Soak grains overnight; drain. Step 2: Pressure cook grains + beef + pastes + spices + oil + water 5–6 whistles. Step 3: Shred beef; mash to thick consistency (add water if needed). Step 4: Fry onions golden; temper with cumin/chilies/garlic. Step 5: Add to haleem; simmer 30 mins. Step 6: Garnish & serve hot with naan. Tip: Slow mash for authentic texture.
nutrition: 450–500 Calories, 20–25g Fat, Protein 30g
health_recommendation: Protein from meat/dal; grains fiber. Rich—occasional; use less oil. Nourishing one-pot meal.

ingredients: 500g chicken cubes, 1 cup cilantro leaves, ½ cup mint leaves, 3–4 green chilies, 4–5 garlic cloves, 1-inch ginger, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp cream, 2 tbsp oil, 2 tbsp peanuts (optional—for creaminess), salt to taste
directions: Step 1: Blend cilantro, mint, chilies, garlic, ginger, peanuts to smooth paste. Step 2: Mix paste with cumin, garam masala, lemon juice, cream, oil, salt. Step 3: Marinate chicken 2 hrs (or overnight). Step 4: Skewer or grill/pan-fry 10–12 mins (turn for even char). Step 5: Baste with marinade. Serve hot with chutney. Tip: Green marinade key.
nutrition: 320–360 Calories, 18–22g Fat, Protein 30–35g
health_recommendation: Chicken protein; herbs antioxidants/digestion. Yogurt/cream calcium. Grilled healthier—great appetizer.

ingredients: 4 tbsp soy sauce (regular or low-sodium), 2 tbsp peanut butter (smooth, natural—for thickness & nutty flavor), 1 tbsp honey or brown sugar (for sweetness), 2 tsp rice vinegar or white vinegar (for tang), 1 tsp sesame oil (for aroma), 1 tsp chili garlic sauce or sriracha (adjust for heat), ½ tsp garlic powder, ½ tsp five-spice powder (optional—for authentic depth), 1–2 tbsp water (to thin)
directions: Step 1: In a small saucepan, combine 4 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp peanut butter, 1 tbsp honey/brown sugar, 2 tsp rice vinegar, 1 tsp sesame oil, 1 tsp chili garlic sauce, ½ tsp garlic powder, and ½ tsp five-spice powder (if using). Step 2: Heat over low-medium, stirring constantly with whisk or spoon until peanut butter melts and mixture is smooth (3–5 mins). Step 3: Add 1–2 tbsp water gradually while stirring to reach desired thickness (should be glossy & pourable like store-bought hoisin). Step 4: Taste & adjust: more honey for sweet, vinegar for tang, chili for heat. Step 5: Cool completely. Store in airtight jar in fridge up to 2 weeks. Stir before use. Tip: Whisk vigorously to avoid lumps.
nutrition: 60–80 Calories, 3–4g Fat, Sugar 5–6g, Sodium 500–600mg
health_recommendation: Lower sodium than store-bought; peanut butter adds healthy fats/protein. Moderate sugar—use less or sugar substitute. Versatile sauce—great for stir-fries, marinades, dipping. Healthier homemade alternative.

recipe_name: Homemade Tomato Ketchup From Fresh Tomatoes
ingredients: 1.5 kg ripe tomatoes (chopped—use Roma or fleshy varieties for thick consistency), ½ cup sugar (100g—adjust for sweetness), ¼ cup white vinegar or apple cider vinegar (for tang & preservation), 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp black pepper powder, ½ tsp red chili flakes or paprika (for mild heat/color), ½ tsp garlic powder, ½ tsp onion powder, ½ tsp celery seeds (optional—for classic flavor), 1 small cinnamon stick (optional), 2–3 cloves (optional)
directions: Step 1: Wash & roughly chop 1.5 kg tomatoes (no need to peel—skins soften). Step 2: In large pot, cook tomatoes on medium until soft & mushy (15–20 mins—stir occasionally). Step 3: Blend cooked tomatoes to smooth puree (immersion blender or regular blender—strain through sieve for smoother texture if desired). Step 4: Return puree to pot; add sugar, vinegar, salt, pepper, chili flakes/paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, celery seeds, cinnamon stick, cloves. Step 5: Simmer on low-medium 30–40 mins, stirring every 5–7 mins (mixture thickens—reduce to half volume; it should coat spoon). Step 6: Taste & adjust sugar/vinegar/salt. Remove cinnamon/cloves. Cool completely. Step 7: Pour into sterilized bottle/jar. Refrigerate up to 1 month. Tip: Longer simmer = thicker ketchup.
nutrition: 40–50 Calories per 2 tbsp, Sugar 8–10g, Sodium 200–250mg
health_recommendation: Tomatoes lycopene (antioxidant/heart health); vinegar aids digestion. No preservatives—healthier than store-bought. High sugar—reduce to ⅓ cup. Natural, flavorful condiment—great for dips, sandwiches.

ingredients: 24–28 Oreo cookies (crushed—leave some chunky for texture), 2 cups (500ml) heavy whipping cream (chilled), 1 can (400g) sweetened condensed milk (for sweetness & creaminess), 1 tsp vanilla extract (for flavor), pinch salt (enhances sweetness)
directions: Step 1: Crush 24–28 Oreos (leave some larger pieces for crunch). Step 2: In chilled bowl, whip 2 cups heavy cream to stiff peaks (3–4 mins with electric beater). Step 3: Gently fold in condensed milk, vanilla, and salt until smooth (don’t deflate whipped cream). Step 4: Fold in crushed Oreos (reserve some for top). Step 5: Pour into freezer-safe container; smooth top; sprinkle reserved Oreos. Step 6: Cover with plastic wrap (touching surface); freeze 6–8 hrs or overnight. Step 7: Let soften 5–10 mins before scooping. Serve. Tip: No churn—whipped cream gives light texture.
nutrition: 380–420 Calories, 25–28g Fat, Sugar 30–35g
health_recommendation: Oreos add carbs/sugar; cream fat. High calorie—occasional treat. Healthier: use low-fat condensed milk or Greek yogurt swap, reduce Oreos. Fun, creamy dessert—great for parties.

ingredients: 1 cup granulated sugar (200g), 6 tbsp unsalted butter (cut into pieces), ½ cup heavy cream (room temp), 1 tsp sea salt or fleur de sel (adjust for saltiness), 1 tsp vanilla extract (optional)
directions: Step 1: In heavy saucepan, heat sugar on medium, stirring constantly until melts to amber liquid (5–7 mins—don’t burn). Step 2: Remove from heat; add butter pieces one by one (careful—it bubbles vigorously); stir until smooth. Step 3: Slowly pour in cream (bubbles a lot); stir until combined. Step 4: Add salt & vanilla; stir. Step 5: Cool 10–15 mins (thickens). Pour into jar. Refrigerate up to 2 weeks (reheat gently). Tip: Watch sugar closely—medium heat prevents burning.
nutrition: 100–120 Calories per 2 tbsp, Fat 7–8g, Sugar 15–18g
health_recommendation: Sugar caramelized for flavor; cream calcium. High sugar/fat—small drizzle only. Rich topping—use sparingly on desserts.

ingredients: 1 can (400g) sweetened condensed milk (full-fat for best texture), water (enough to cover can)
directions: Step 1: Remove label from can. Place unopened can in deep pot. Step 2: Cover with water (2–3 inches above can). Step 3: Bring to boil on high; reduce to low simmer. Step 4: Simmer 2.5–3 hrs (check water every 30 mins—add boiling water to keep covered). Step 5: Remove can with tongs; cool completely (unopened—at least 2 hrs). Step 6: Open can—caramelized dulce de leche ready. Use in desserts. Store refrigerated 2–3 weeks. Tip: Don’t open hot—pressure causes explosion.
nutrition: 130–150 Calories per 2 tbsp, Sugar 20–22g
health_recommendation: Milk calcium/protein; caramelized sugar flavor. High sugar—small amounts. Homemade caramel—better than store-bought.

ingredients: 1 can (400g) chickpeas (drained & rinsed—reserve liquid), 2–3 tbsp olive oil (for smoothness), 2–3 tbsp lemon juice (adjust tartness), 2 garlic cloves (minced), ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp paprika (optional), salt to taste, 3–5 tbsp chickpea liquid/aquafaba (for creamy texture), fresh coriander or parsley (optional garnish)
directions: Step 1: In blender/food processor, add chickpeas, garlic, lemon juice, cumin, paprika, salt. Step 2: Blend until chunky. Step 3: Add olive oil & chickpea liquid 1 tbsp at a time while blending until smooth & creamy (2–3 mins). Step 4: Taste & adjust salt/lemon/cumin. Step 5: Transfer to bowl; drizzle oil, sprinkle paprika/coriander. Step 6: Serve with veggies/pita. Tip: More liquid = creamier.
nutrition: 120–140 Calories per ¼ cup, Fat 7–9g, Protein 5g, Fiber 5g
health_recommendation: Chickpeas protein/fiber (digestion/satiety); olive oil healthy fats. No tahini—lighter. Very healthy dip—great snack/appetizer.

ingredients: 200g dried plums/aloo bukhara (pitted), 100g tamarind/imli (seedless), 1 cup sugar (adjust), ½ tsp black salt, ½ tsp roasted cumin powder, ¼ tsp black pepper, 4–5 cups water, mint leaves & ice for serving
directions: Step 1: Soak aloo bukhara & tamarind in 2 cups warm water 30 mins. Step 2: Mash & strain (squeeze well—discard pulp). Step 3: In pan, combine extract, sugar, black salt, cumin powder, black pepper, 2–3 cups water. Step 4: Boil 10–15 mins until sugar dissolves & slightly thickens. Step 5: Cool completely; chill 2 hrs. Step 6: Serve over ice with mint. Tip: Adjust sugar—tart-sweet balance.
nutrition: 120–150 Calories per glass, Sugar 25–30g
health_recommendation: Tamarind/plums digestion/vitamin C; hydrating sharbat. High sugar—reduce. Refreshing summer drink.

ingredients: 1 kg chicken (boneless thighs), 1 cup yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp garam masala, salt, 4 tbsp butter, 2 medium onions pureed, 4 tomatoes pureed, 1 tbsp cashew paste (soaked & ground), 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder, ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp sugar, ½ cup cream, 1 tsp kasuri methi, coriander for garnish
directions: Step 1: Marinate chicken with yogurt, ginger-garlic, chili powder, garam masala, salt 1 hr. Step 2: Grill or pan-fry chicken 8–10 mins (charred outside). Step 3: Heat 4 tbsp butter; sauté onion puree 8–10 mins golden. Step 4: Add tomato puree, cashew paste, spices, sugar; cook 15–20 mins until oil separates. Step 5: Add grilled chicken; simmer 10 mins. Step 6: Add cream, kasuri methi; simmer 5 mins. Garnish coriander. Serve with naan/rice. Tip: Slow cook gravy for richness.
nutrition: 480–550 Calories, 35–40g Fat, Protein 35–40g
health_recommendation: Chicken protein; yogurt probiotics; nuts healthy fats. Creamy—rich dish; use less butter/cream. Classic comfort curry.

ingredients: 500g ground chicken (minced breast/thigh), 1 medium onion finely chopped & squeezed dry, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1–2 green chilies chopped, 1 tsp red chili flakes, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp black pepper, salt to taste, 1 egg (for binding), 2–3 tbsp bread crumbs, 1 tbsp lemon juice, oil for frying
directions: Step 1: Mix ground chicken with onion, ginger-garlic, chilies, spices, salt, lemon juice, egg, bread crumbs. Step 2: Knead well; chill 30 mins (firms). Step 3: Shape into patties (½ inch thick). Step 4: Heat oil; shallow fry 5–6 mins per side until golden & cooked (165°F internal). Step 5: Serve in buns with veggies/sauce. Tip: Don’t overmix—juicy patties.
nutrition: 250–280 Calories per patty, 15–18g Fat, Protein 22–25g
health_recommendation: Chicken protein; low carb. Fried—bake/air-fry healthier. Great burger base—add veggies.

ingredients: 500g chicken/mutton liver (cleaned & cubed), ½ cup yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp green chili paste, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp garam masala, salt to taste, 3–4 tbsp oil, 1 medium onion sliced, 1 tomato chopped, 2–3 green chilies slit, fresh coriander, lemon juice
directions: Step 1: Marinate liver with yogurt, ginger-garlic, chili paste, dry spices, salt 30 mins. Step 2: Heat oil; fry onion golden (5–7 mins). Step 3: Add tomato; cook 5 mins. Step 4: Add marinated liver; stir-fry high 8–10 mins (don’t overcook—tender inside). Step 5: Add slit chilies; cook 2 mins. Step 6: Finish with lemon juice & coriander. Serve hot. Tip: High heat quick cook—soft liver.
nutrition: 320–360 Calories, 20–24g Fat, Protein 28–32g, Iron high
health_recommendation: Liver iron/vitamin A (blood/energy); protein-rich. Quick, nutrient-dense dish—occasional (high cholesterol).

ingredients: 4 cups water, 4–5 tsp black tea leaves (strong CTC like Tapal/Kenya—for robust flavor), 1–1½ cups full-fat milk (or evaporated milk for extra creaminess), 4–6 tsp sugar (adjust to taste—karak is sweet), 4–5 green cardamoms (crushed—for aroma), 1-inch cinnamon stick (optional), 2–3 cloves (optional), ½ tsp fennel seeds (saunf—optional for digestive note), pinch ginger powder or ½ tsp grated fresh ginger (for warmth)
directions: Step 1: In a saucepan, add 4 cups water + crushed cardamoms + cinnamon + cloves + fennel seeds + ginger. Bring to boil on high (5 mins—spices infuse). Step 2: Add 4–5 tsp black tea leaves; boil 2–3 mins (strong brew—karak color darkens). Step 3: Add 1–1½ cups milk + 4–6 tsp sugar; stir well. Step 4: Bring back to rolling boil; simmer 3–5 mins (color turns caramel-brown—stir occasionally to prevent overflow). Step 5: Strain into cups (or pull/pour from height for froth—traditional karak style). Step 6: Serve hot. Tip: Boil longer for stronger taste; adjust milk/sugar to preference.
nutrition: 80–120 Calories per cup, Sugar 10–15g, Fat 3–5g (from milk)
health_recommendation: Black tea antioxidants (heart health); milk calcium/protein. High sugar—reduce for lighter. Energizing, comforting chai—great for cold days. Healthier: use low-fat milk, less sugar.

ingredients: 1 kg chicken (bone-in pieces—thighs/legs for flavor), 1 cup thick yogurt, 2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tbsp green chili paste, 2 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder (for color & mild heat), 1 tsp fennel powder (saunf—for Kashmiri aroma), ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp garam masala, 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp black pepper powder, 4–5 tbsp oil/ghee, 2 medium onions finely sliced, 2 tomatoes pureed, ½ cup milk or cream (for richness), 1 tsp kasuri methi (crushed), ginger juliennes & coriander for garnish
directions: Step 1: Marinate chicken with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, green chili paste, Kashmiri chili, fennel powder, turmeric, coriander powder, garam masala, salt, black pepper 1–2 hrs (or overnight). Step 2: Heat 4–5 tbsp oil/ghee; fry onions golden-brown 10–12 mins (stir often). Step 3: Add marinated chicken; stir-fry high 8–10 mins until color changes. Step 4: Add tomato puree; cook 8–10 mins until oil separates. Step 5: Add ½–¾ cup water; cover & simmer low 25–30 mins until tender & gravy thick. Step 6: Add milk/cream, kasuri methi; simmer 5 mins. Garnish ginger/coriander. Serve with naan/rice. Tip: Low heat after yogurt prevents curdling.
nutrition: 420–480 Calories, 28–32g Fat, Protein 35–40g
health_recommendation: Chicken protein; yogurt probiotics; fennel digestion. Moderate oil—use less ghee. Aromatic, mild curry—great family dish.

ingredients: Dough: 3 cups flour, 1 tsp salt, 1 tbsp sugar, 1 tsp instant yeast, ½ cup warm milk, 1 egg, 2 tbsp oil/butter, water as needed. Filling: 400g ground meat (mutton/beef), 1 medium onion finely chopped & squeezed dry, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tbsp green chili paste, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, salt, 2 tbsp oil, 2 tbsp chopped coriander
directions: Step 1: Activate yeast in warm milk + sugar 5–10 mins. Step 2: Mix flour, salt, egg, oil; add yeast mix; knead soft dough (8–10 mins). Rise 1 hr. Step 3: Heat oil; sauté ginger-garlic + chili paste 1 min. Step 4: Add keema; cook 10–12 mins until brown & dry. Step 5: Add spices, onion, coriander; cook 5 mins. Cool. Step 6: Divide dough; roll small circle; place filling in center; seal; roll gently. Step 7: Cook on hot tawa 2–3 mins per side; brush butter. Bake 220°C 8–10 mins for oven version. Tip: Seal well—filling stays in.
nutrition: 350–400 Calories per naan, 15–18g Fat, Protein 18–22g
health_recommendation: Chicken/mutton protein; dough carbs. Rich—bake instead of fry. Stuffed bread—great meal/snack.

ingredients: Filling: 400g ground meat, 1 tbsp ginger paste, 1 tbsp garlic paste, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tbsp green chili paste, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp red chili flakes, ½ tsp black pepper, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, salt, 2 tbsp chopped coriander, 1 onion finely chopped, 2–3 green chilies chopped. Dough: 2 cups flour, ½ tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, water. Oil for frying
directions: Step 1: Heat oil; sauté ginger-garlic + chili paste 1 min. Step 2: Add keema; cook 8–10 mins until browned. Step 3: Add spices, onion, chilies, coriander, lemon juice; cook 5–7 mins until dry. Cool. Step 4: Knead dough soft; rest 30 mins. Step 5: Roll oval; cut half; shape cone; fill keema; seal. Step 6: Fry medium-low 6–8 mins golden-crisp. Serve with chutney. Tip: Low heat—crisp outside, cooked inside.
nutrition: 250–300 Calories per samosa, 15–18g Fat, Protein 12–15g
health_recommendation: Meat protein; dough carbs. Fried—bake/air-fry healthier. Popular snack—occasional.

ingredients: 1 kg ripe kiwis (peeled & chopped—about 6–8 large kiwis), 600–800g granulated sugar (adjust to 60–80% kiwi weight), 4–5 tbsp fresh lemon juice (for tartness & setting), pinch salt (enhances flavor)
directions: Step 1: Peel & chop kiwis (discard hard core if any). Step 2: In heavy pan, combine kiwis, sugar, lemon juice, salt. Let sit 30 mins (releases juices). Step 3: Bring to boil medium; reduce low-medium; simmer 30–40 mins, stirring often (skim foam). Step 4: Mash lightly for chunky or blend smooth. Test: cold plate—wrinkles when pushed. Step 5: Cool; jar. Refrigerate 3–4 weeks. Tip: No pectin—long simmer sets.
nutrition: 50–60 Calories per tbsp, Sugar 10–12g
health_recommendation: Kiwi vitamin C/fiber; no added pectin—natural. High sugar—reduce. Healthy homemade jam—great spread.

ingredients: 1 kg mutton/goat meat (bone-in pieces—shoulder/leg for flavor & tenderness), ½ cup oil/ghee (or half each for richness), 6–8 medium tomatoes (chopped or pureed—fresh for tang & gravy base), 8–10 green chilies slit (adjust for heat), 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp cumin seeds, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp red chili powder (adjust), 1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder (for color), ½ tsp turmeric powder, 1–1½ tsp salt (adjust), 1 tsp black pepper powder (freshly crushed—for punch), 1 tsp garam masala (added at end), 1 tbsp julienned ginger (for garnish), 2–3 tbsp fresh coriander chopped (for garnish), 1 tsp dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi—crushed, optional)
directions: Step 1: Clean mutton pieces; pat dry (removes excess moisture for better frying). Step 2: Heat ½ cup oil/ghee in heavy karahi/wok on high. Add cumin seeds; crackle 20–30 secs. Step 3: Add mutton pieces; stir-fry on high 8–12 mins until color changes & water evaporates (stir frequently—seals juices). Step 4: Add ginger-garlic paste; sauté 1–2 mins until raw smell disappears. Step 5: Add chopped tomatoes, all dry spices (coriander, red chili, Kashmiri chili, turmeric, salt); cook on medium-high 12–15 mins until tomatoes break down, oil separates, and masala thickens (bhuno technique—stir often, add splash water if sticking). Step 6: Add ½–¾ cup water (enough to cover half meat); cover & simmer on low 30–40 mins until mutton tender & gravy semi-dry (stir midway; add water sparingly if needed). Step 7: Uncover; increase heat to medium-high; add slit green chilies, black pepper powder, garam masala, kasuri methi (crush between palms); cook 5–8 mins until oil floats & gravy clings to meat. Step 8: Garnish with ginger juliennes & coriander. Serve hot with naan/kulcha/roti. Tip: High heat initially for flavor; low simmer for tenderness. Don’t add water excessively—authentic karahi is semi-dry.
nutrition: 480–550 Calories, 35–40g Fat (65–75%), Saturated Fat 12–15g (60–75%), Protein 35–40g (70–80%), Sodium 900–1100mg
health_recommendation: High protein from mutton (muscle repair, satiety); iron from meat (blood health). Spices (cumin, turmeric) anti-inflammatory/digestion aid. High fat from ghee/oil—healthier by using ¼ cup oil & lean cuts. Classic Pakistani restaurant-style curry—great for special occasions; pair with salad/veggies for fiber balance.

ingredients: 1 whole mutton leg (raan—2–2.5 kg, cleaned & trimmed), 1 cup thick yogurt, 2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 2 tbsp green chili paste, 2 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder (color), 1 tsp black pepper powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp turmeric, 1–1½ tsp salt, 2 tbsp lemon juice, ¼ cup oil/ghee, 2 tbsp papaya paste (optional tenderizer), 1 tsp chaat masala (for finishing), ginger juliennes, fresh coriander, lemon wedges for garnish
directions: Step 1: Clean mutton leg; make deep slits all over (helps marinade penetrate). Step 2: Mix yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, green chili paste, all dry spices (red chili, Kashmiri chili, black pepper, garam masala, cumin, coriander, turmeric), salt, lemon juice, oil/ghee, papaya paste (if using). Rub marinade generously into slits & all over leg. Step 3: Cover & marinate in fridge 4–8 hrs (or overnight for best tenderness & flavor). Step 4: Preheat oven to 180°C/350°F (or use heavy pot/pressure cooker). Place marinated raan in greased baking tray or pot. Step 5: Roast in oven 2–2.5 hrs (or pressure cook 5–6 whistles then slow roast) until tender & golden (baste with marinade/oil every 30 mins; cover with foil first hour to retain moisture). Step 6: Increase heat to 200°C last 15–20 mins for crispy exterior (or broil/grill). Step 7: Rest 10–15 mins; sprinkle chaat masala. Garnish ginger juliennes, coriander, lemon wedges. Serve hot with naan/rice. Tip: Slow roast for fall-off-bone texture; check tenderness with fork.
nutrition: 450–500 Calories, 30–35g Fat, Protein 40–45g, Iron high
health_recommendation: High protein/iron from mutton (energy/blood health); yogurt probiotics/tenderizes. Collagen from bones (joints/skin). Rich—occasional; use less oil, lean leg. Festive showstopper—pair with salad.

ingredients: Base: 200g digestive biscuits (crushed), 100g butter (melted). Mousse: 2 cups heavy whipping cream (chilled), 1 can (400g) sweetened condensed milk, ¾ cup fresh lemon juice (from 4–5 lemons—for bright tang), zest of 2 lemons (for flavor), 1 tbsp gelatin powder (bloomed in ¼ cup cold water—for setting), 1 tsp vanilla essence, yellow food color (optional), whipped cream & lemon slices for garnish
directions: Step 1: Crush biscuits; mix with melted butter. Press firmly into 8-inch springform pan base. Chill 20 mins. Step 2: Bloom gelatin: Sprinkle 1 tbsp gelatin over ¼ cup cold water; let stand 5 mins. Microwave 10 secs or heat gently until dissolved. Step 3: Whip 2 cups chilled cream to stiff peaks (3–4 mins). Step 4: In bowl, whisk condensed milk, lemon juice, lemon zest, vanilla until smooth. Add dissolved gelatin; mix quickly. Step 5: Fold whipped cream into lemon mixture gently (retain air for light texture). Step 6: Pour over chilled base; smooth top. Chill 4–6 hrs or overnight to set. Step 7: Run knife around edges; remove springform. Garnish with whipped cream & lemon slices. Serve chilled. Tip: Fold gently—overmixing deflates mousse.
nutrition: 380–420 Calories, 25–28g Fat, Sugar 30–35g
health_recommendation: Lemon vitamin C (immunity); condensed milk calcium. High sugar/fat—occasional treat; use low-fat condensed milk or Greek yogurt swap. Refreshing no-bake cake—great for summers.

ingredients: 2 cups heavy whipping cream (chilled), 1 can (400g) sweetened condensed milk, ¾–1 cup fresh lemon juice (from 6–8 lemons—adjust tartness), zest of 3–4 lemons (for bright flavor), 1 tsp vanilla extract, pinch salt (enhances sweetness)
directions: Step 1: Zest 3–4 lemons; squeeze juice (¾–1 cup). Step 2: In large bowl, whisk condensed milk, lemon juice, lemon zest, vanilla, salt until smooth. Step 3: Whip chilled cream to stiff peaks (3–4 mins). Step 4: Fold whipped cream into lemon mixture gently (retain air). Step 5: Pour into freezer-safe container; cover with plastic wrap (touching surface). Freeze 6–8 hrs or overnight. Step 6: Let soften 5–10 mins before scooping. Serve. Tip: No churn—whipped cream gives light texture.
nutrition: 320–360 Calories, 22–25g Fat, Sugar 30–35g
health_recommendation: Lemon vitamin C; cream fat. High sugar/fat—occasional; use less condensed milk. Refreshing summer treat—natural citrus flavor.

ingredients: 1 kg ripe mangoes (peeled, pitted & chopped—Alphonso or sweet variety), 600–800g granulated sugar (adjust to 60–80% mango weight), 4–5 tbsp fresh lemon juice, pinch salt, ½ tsp cardamom powder (optional for aroma)
directions: Step 1: Peel/pit/chop mangoes; mash lightly. Step 2: In pan, combine mangoes, sugar, lemon juice, salt. Let sit 30 mins. Step 3: Boil medium; reduce low-medium; simmer 35–45 mins, stirring often (mash for smooth or leave chunky). Step 4: Test on cold plate—wrinkles when pushed. Step 5: Add cardamom (if using); cool; jar. Refrigerate 3–4 weeks. Tip: No pectin—long simmer sets.
nutrition: 50–60 Calories per tbsp, Sugar 10–12g
health_recommendation: Mango vitamin A/C; fiber. High sugar—reduce. Natural homemade jam—great spread.

ingredients: 1 liter full-fat milk, 1 cup mango puree (from 2–3 ripe mangoes—Alphonso best), ½–¾ cup sugar (adjust), ½ cup condensed milk (for creaminess), ½ tsp cardamom powder, pinch saffron (soaked), 2 tbsp chopped pistachios/almonds, 1 tsp rose water (optional)
directions: Step 1: Boil milk on medium; simmer 20–30 mins, stirring/scraping cream (reduce to half). Step 2: Add sugar, condensed milk, cardamom, saffron; stir until dissolved. Cool. Step 3: Mix in mango puree, rose water, nuts. Step 4: Pour into kulfi molds or cups; insert sticks. Step 5: Freeze 6–8 hrs or overnight. Step 6: Run under warm water to unmold. Serve. Tip: Simmer milk slowly—creamy texture.
nutrition: 250–300 Calories per kulfi, Fat 12–15g, Sugar 25–30g
health_recommendation: Mango vitamin A/C; milk calcium/protein. Creamy—occasional; use low-fat milk. Refreshing summer dessert.

ingredients: 200g digestive biscuits (crushed), ½ cup butter (melted), ½ cup cocoa powder, 1 cup powdered sugar, ½ cup milk, 1 tsp vanilla essence, ½ cup chopped nuts (walnuts/almonds), 100g dark chocolate (melted—for coating, optional)
directions: Step 1: Crush biscuits coarsely. Step 2: In pan, melt butter; add cocoa, powdered sugar, milk, vanilla; cook low 3–5 mins until smooth. Step 3: Remove from heat; mix in crushed biscuits & nuts. Step 4: Press into greased loaf tin or shape into log. Step 5: Drizzle melted chocolate; chill 4–6 hrs. Step 6: Slice & serve. Tip: No-bake—easy.
nutrition: 300–350 Calories per slice, Fat 18–22g, Sugar 20–25g
health_recommendation: Biscuits carbs; nuts healthy fats. High sugar/fat—occasional. No-bake treat—great for kids.

ingredients: 2 medium potatoes (cubed), 1 medium carrot (sliced), 1 cup cauliflower florets, ½ cup green beans (chopped), ½ cup green peas, 1 medium onion (sliced), 2 medium tomatoes (chopped), 3–4 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin seeds, ½ tsp fenugreek seeds (optional), 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp green chili paste, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp turmeric powder, 1 tsp red chili powder (adjust), 1 tsp salt (adjust), ½ tsp garam masala, 1 tsp kasuri methi (crushed), fresh coriander for garnish
directions: Step 1: Wash & chop all vegetables uniformly (medium size for even cooking). Step 2: Heat 3–4 tbsp oil in kadai on medium. Add cumin seeds & fenugreek seeds; crackle 20–30 secs. Step 3: Add sliced onion; fry 5–7 mins until golden. Step 4: Add ginger-garlic paste & green chili paste; sauté 1 min until raw smell goes. Step 5: Add tomatoes, coriander powder, turmeric, red chili powder, salt; cook 8–10 mins until tomatoes soft & oil separates. Step 6: Add all vegetables; stir-fry high 3–4 mins to coat masala. Step 7: Add ½–¾ cup water; cover & simmer low 15–20 mins until veggies tender (stir midway—don’t overcook). Step 8: Uncover; add garam masala & kasuri methi; cook 2–3 mins until dry & oil floats. Garnish coriander. Serve hot with roti/rice. Tip: High heat initially seals flavor; low simmer keeps veggies crisp-tender.
nutrition: 180–220 Calories, 12–15g Fat, Protein 5–6g, Fiber 6–8g
health_recommendation: Mixed vegetables high fiber/vitamins (A/C/K from carrots/cauliflower); low calorie. Spices digestion/anti-inflammatory. Very healthy sabzi—use minimal oil (2–3 tbsp). Pair with dal/roti for balanced meal. Everyday comfort food.

ingredients: 2 cups grated carrots (about 4–5 medium carrots—fresh & juicy), 1½ cups all-purpose flour, 1 cup granulated sugar, ½ cup brown sugar, ¾ cup vegetable oil (for moisture), 3 large eggs, 1 tsp baking powder, 1 tsp baking soda, ½ tsp salt, 1 tsp cinnamon powder, ½ tsp nutmeg powder, 1 tsp vanilla essence, ½ cup chopped walnuts (optional), ½ cup raisins (optional)
directions: Step 1: Preheat oven to 180°C/350°F. Grease & flour bundt pan (or line with parchment). Step 2: In large bowl, whisk 3 eggs, 1 cup sugar, ½ cup brown sugar, ¾ cup oil, 1 tsp vanilla until smooth & pale (2–3 mins). Step 3: Sift flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, nutmeg together. Step 4: Fold dry ingredients into wet mixture gently until just combined (don’t overmix). Step 5: Fold in grated carrots, walnuts, raisins (if using). Step 6: Pour batter into prepared bundt pan; tap gently to remove air bubbles. Step 7: Bake 50–60 mins (toothpick in center comes out clean). Cool in pan 10 mins; invert onto rack; cool completely. Step 8: Optional: Dust with powdered sugar or glaze (powdered sugar + milk + vanilla). Tip: Don’t overbake—moist texture key.
nutrition: 320–360 Calories per slice, 18–22g Fat, Sugar 25–30g
health_recommendation: Carrots vitamin A (eye/skin health); nuts healthy fats. Moderate sugar/fat—reduce to ¾ cup sugar total. Moist, spiced cake—great tea-time treat.

ingredients: Kofta: 500g ground meat (beef/mutton), 1 medium onion grated & squeezed dry, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tbsp green chili paste, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp red chili powder, salt, 2 tbsp gram flour (besan—for binding), 1 egg (optional), chopped coriander. Curry: 3 tbsp oil, 2 medium onions sliced, 2 tomatoes pureed, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder, 1 tsp garam masala, salt, ½ cup yogurt, 1 cup water, fresh coriander
directions: Step 1: Mix kofta ingredients; shape into small balls (20–24). Step 2: Heat oil; fry koftas golden (optional—can add raw to curry). Step 3: In same pan, fry onions golden (8–10 mins). Step 4: Add ginger-garlic paste; sauté 1 min. Step 5: Add tomato puree, spices, salt; cook 10–12 mins until oil separates. Step 6: Whisk yogurt; add slowly; cook 5 mins. Step 7: Add water; bring to boil. Add koftas; simmer 15–20 mins until cooked & gravy thick. Step 8: Garnish coriander. Serve with rice/roti. Tip: Add koftas gently—don’t stir much.
nutrition: 380–420 Calories, 25–28g Fat, Protein 30–35g
health_recommendation: Meat protein; gram flour fiber. Rich curry—use less oil/yogurt. Comforting family dish.

ingredients: 500g chicken/mutton (bone-in), 2 cups basmati rice (soaked 30 mins), ½ cup oil/ghee, 2 medium onions sliced, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 4–5 green chilies slit, 1 cup yogurt, 1 tbsp fennel seeds (saunf), 1 tsp black peppercorns, 4–5 cloves, 4 green cardamoms, 1-inch cinnamon, 1 black cardamom, 1 bay leaf, 1 tsp coriander seeds, salt to taste, 4–5 cups yakhni (stock from meat), fresh coriander & mint for garnish
directions: Step 1: Boil meat with whole spices (fennel, peppercorns, cloves, cardamoms, cinnamon, bay leaf, coriander seeds), salt, 5–6 cups water until tender (pressure cook 3–4 whistles or simmer 45–60 mins). Strain yakhni (stock); shred meat lightly. Step 2: Heat ½ cup oil/ghee; fry onions golden (10–12 mins). Step 3: Add ginger-garlic paste & green chilies; sauté 1 min. Step 4: Add yogurt; cook 5 mins until oil separates. Step 5: Add shredded meat & yakhni (measure 4–5 cups); bring to boil. Step 6: Add soaked & drained rice; stir gently. Cook high 5 mins, then low covered 15–20 mins until rice done & fluffy. Step 7: Fluff with fork; garnish coriander/mint. Serve hot. Tip: Use yakhni instead of plain water for flavor.
nutrition: 450–500 Calories, 20–25g Fat, Protein 25–30g
health_recommendation: Meat protein; rice carbs. Aromatic & light—healthier pulao (less oil). Great one-pot meal—pair with raita.

ingredients: 2 tbsp fennel seeds, 2 tbsp cumin seeds, 1 tbsp coriander seeds, 1 tbsp black peppercorns, 8–10 cloves, 4–5 green cardamoms, 2 black cardamoms, 1-inch cinnamon stick, 1 star anise, 4–5 button red chilies, 1 tsp nigella seeds (kalonji), 1 tsp poppy seeds, 1 tsp dried ginger powder, ½ tsp turmeric powder, salt to taste, 1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder (optional for color)
directions: Step 1: Dry roast whole spices (fennel, cumin, coriander, peppercorns, cloves, cardamoms, cinnamon, star anise, red chilies, nigella, poppy seeds) on low-medium 4–6 mins until fragrant (stir constantly—don’t burn). Step 2: Cool completely. Step 3: Grind to fine powder with salt, ginger powder, turmeric, Kashmiri chili (if using). Step 4: Store airtight. Use 2–3 tbsp per 1 kg meat in nihari. Tip: Fresh roast = best aroma.
nutrition: N/A (spice mix)
health_recommendation: Spices anti-inflammatory/digestion (fennel, ginger); low calorie. Use sparingly—flavorful base.

ingredients: 1 kg beef/mutton (shank/shin bone-in), ½ cup oil/ghee, 2 large onions sliced, 2–3 tbsp nihari masala (from above or store-bought), 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tbsp green chili paste, salt to taste, ½ cup wheat flour slurry (for thickening), ½ cup yogurt (optional), garnish: ginger juliennes, green chilies, coriander, lemon wedges
directions: Step 1: Heat oil/ghee; fry onions golden (10–12 mins). Step 2: Add ginger-garlic paste, green chili paste; sauté 1–2 mins. Step 3: Add meat; fry 8–10 mins. Step 4: Add nihari masala, salt; stir 2 mins. Step 5: Add water (6–8 cups); pressure cook 5–6 whistles (or simmer 3–4 hrs) until tender & broth rich. Step 6: Add wheat flour slurry; simmer 10–15 mins to thicken. Step 7: Optional: temper with ghee + chili powder. Garnish ginger/chilies/coriander/lemon. Serve hot with naan. Tip: Slow cook for gelatinous texture.
nutrition: 450–500 Calories, 30–35g Fat, Protein 35–40g
health_recommendation: Protein/collagen from bones (joints/skin); iron. Rich—occasional; use less oil. Traditional slow-cooked comfort food.

ingredients: 36–40 Oreo cookies (full pack ~400g—use original for classic flavor), ¾–1 cup sweetened condensed milk (start with ¾ cup—adjust for stickiness), 100g milk or dark chocolate (melted—for coating, optional), 1 tsp vanilla essence (optional—for extra flavor), desiccated coconut or crushed Oreos (for rolling—optional)
directions: Step 1: Crush 36–40 Oreo cookies (with cream filling) to fine crumbs (food processor or rolling pin in zip bag—fine texture helps binding). Step 2: In large bowl, add crushed Oreos + ¾ cup condensed milk + vanilla essence (if using). Mix with spoon or hands until dough forms (should be sticky but hold shape—if too dry, add 1–2 tbsp more condensed milk; if too wet, add 1–2 tbsp extra crumbs). Step 3: Grease hands lightly with oil/butter; roll mixture into 1-inch balls (about 20–25 pieces). Step 4: Place balls on parchment-lined tray; chill in fridge 30–60 mins to firm. Step 5: Melt 100g chocolate (double boiler or microwave in 20-sec bursts—stir until smooth). Step 6: Dip each chilled ball in melted chocolate (or drizzle over); roll in desiccated coconut or extra crushed Oreos if desired. Step 7: Place back on tray; chill 30 mins to set chocolate. Store in airtight container in fridge up to 1 week. Serve chilled. Tip: Don’t over-chill before coating—hard balls crack chocolate.
nutrition: 120–150 Calories per ball, 6–8g Fat, Sugar 12–15g
health_recommendation: Oreos carbs/sugar; condensed milk calcium/protein. High sugar/fat—small portions only. Fun no-bake treat—great for kids/parties. Healthier tweak: Use dark chocolate (more antioxidants), reduce condensed milk, or skip coating. Energy balls in moderation.

ingredients: 1 kg chicken (bone-in pieces—thighs/legs for flavor), 3 cups basmati rice (soaked 30 mins), ½ cup oil/ghee, 3 large onions thinly sliced, 2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 2 tbsp green chili paste, 1 cup yogurt (whisked), 2 medium tomatoes chopped, 2 tbsp biryani masala (or grind: 1 tsp each cumin, coriander, fennel, 4–5 cloves, 4 green cardamoms, 1 black cardamom, 1-inch cinnamon, 1 bay leaf), 1 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder, ½ tsp turmeric, 2 tsp salt (adjust), ½ tsp garam masala, ¼ cup milk + pinch saffron (soaked), 2–3 boiled eggs (halved—optional), fresh mint & coriander chopped, 2–3 tbsp ghee (for layers), fried onions for garnish
directions: Step 1: Marinate chicken with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, green chili paste, biryani masala, red/Kashmiri chili powder, turmeric, salt 1 hr (or overnight). Step 2: Boil rice in salted water with whole spices (cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaf) until 80% done (6–7 mins); drain & set aside. Step 3: Heat ½ cup oil/ghee; fry onions golden-brown (12–15 mins—stir often). Remove half for garnish. Step 4: In same pot, add marinated chicken; cook high 10–12 mins until color changes. Step 5: Add tomatoes; cook 8–10 mins until soft & oil separates. Step 6: Layer half rice over chicken; sprinkle fried onions, mint, coriander, garam masala, saffron milk, ghee. Add remaining rice; repeat layers. Step 7: Cover tightly (use dough seal or heavy lid); cook on high 5 mins, then low (dum) 20–25 mins. Step 8: Rest 10 mins; mix gently. Garnish eggs & fried onions. Serve hot with raita/salad. Tip: Dum on low—steam cooks rice fully.
nutrition: 550–650 Calories, 25–30g Fat, Protein 30–35g
health_recommendation: Chicken protein; rice carbs. Rich—use less oil/ghee, brown rice for fiber. Classic one-pot meal—pair with yogurt for balance.

ingredients: 1 kg chicken (bone-in thighs/legs), ½ cup oil/ghee, 6–8 medium tomatoes (chopped), 8–10 green chilies slit, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp cumin seeds, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder, ½ tsp turmeric, 1–1½ tsp salt, 1 tsp black pepper powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tbsp julienned ginger, fresh coriander
directions: Step 1: Heat ½ cup oil/ghee in karahi/wok on high. Add cumin seeds; crackle 20 secs. Step 2: Add chicken; stir-fry high 10–12 mins until color changes & water evaporates. Step 3: Add ginger-garlic paste; sauté 1–2 mins. Step 4: Add tomatoes, all dry spices (coriander, red chili, Kashmiri chili, turmeric, salt); cook medium-high 12–15 mins until tomatoes break down & oil separates. Step 5: Add ½ cup water; cover & simmer low 25–30 mins until chicken tender & gravy semi-dry. Step 6: Uncover; add slit green chilies, black pepper, garam masala; cook 5–8 mins until oil floats. Step 7: Garnish ginger juliennes & coriander. Serve hot with naan/roti. Tip: High heat initially; low simmer for flavor.
nutrition: 450–500 Calories, 30–35g Fat, Protein 35–40g
health_recommendation: Protein/iron from chicken; tomatoes vitamin C. High fat—use less oil. Classic restaurant-style curry—pair with salad.

ingredients: 1 kg boneless chicken (thighs—cut cubes), 1 cup thick yogurt, 2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tbsp green chili paste, 2 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp black pepper powder, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tsp salt, 2 tbsp oil, red food color (optional), butter/ghee for basting
directions: Step 1: Clean chicken cubes; make slits. Step 2: Mix yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, green chili paste, all dry spices, lemon juice, salt, oil, food color. Coat chicken well; marinate 2–4 hrs (or overnight). Step 3: Preheat oven 200°C or heat grill pan. Step 4: Thread chicken on skewers or place on tray/pan. Step 5: Grill/bake 20–25 mins (turn & baste with butter/ghee every 8–10 mins) until charred & cooked (165°F internal). Step 6: Rest 5 mins; serve hot with mint chutney/onion rings. Tip: Charcoal grill for smoky flavor.
nutrition: 380–420 Calories, 22–26g Fat, Protein 35–40g
health_recommendation: Chicken protein; yogurt probiotics. Spices metabolism/digestion. Grilled—healthier than fried. Great appetizer/main.

ingredients: 500g ground beef/mutton (minced—20% fat), 1 medium onion finely chopped & squeezed dry, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1–2 green chilies chopped, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp black pepper, salt to taste, 1 egg (beaten—for binding), 2–3 tbsp bread crumbs, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp chopped coriander, oil for shallow frying
directions: Step 1: Mix ground meat with onion, ginger-garlic paste, green chilies, dry spices, salt, lemon juice, coriander. Step 2: Add egg & bread crumbs; knead well. Step 3: Chill mixture 30 mins (firms). Step 4: Shape into oval/round cutlets (½ inch thick). Step 5: Heat oil; shallow fry 4–5 mins per side until golden-crisp & cooked inside. Step 6: Drain; serve hot with chutney/salad. Tip: Don’t overmix—juicy cutlets.
nutrition: 280–320 Calories per cutlet, 20–24g Fat, Protein 18–22g
health_recommendation: Meat protein/iron; bread crumbs carbs. Fried—bake/air-fry healthier. Protein-rich snack/breakfast.

ingredients: 500g ground beef/mutton, ½ cup chana dal (soaked 1 hr), 1 medium onion chopped, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tbsp green chili paste, 1 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, ½ tsp cumin powder, ½ tsp garam masala, salt, 1 egg (beaten—for binding), 2 tbsp gram flour (besan—if needed), oil for shallow frying
directions: Step 1: Boil ground meat + soaked chana dal + onion, ginger-garlic paste, green chili paste, spices, salt with 2 cups water until dry & soft (pressure cook 3–4 whistles). Step 2: Cool; grind to smooth paste (no lumps). Step 3: Mix in beaten egg & gram flour (if sticky). Step 4: Chill 1 hr. Step 5: Shape into patties; shallow fry 3–4 mins per side golden-crisp. Step 6: Serve with chutney. Tip: Boil dry—prevents soggy kababs.
nutrition: 220–260 Calories per kabab, 15–18g Fat, Protein 15–18g
health_recommendation: Meat protein; dal fiber/protein. Fried—bake healthier. Classic snack—pair with salad.

yield: 1 kg ripe papaya (peeled, deseeded & chopped—use very ripe for natural sweetness & thick texture), 600–800g granulated sugar (adjust to 60–80% papaya weight—start lower), 4–5 tbsp fresh lemon juice (for tartness & natural pectin setting), pinch salt (enhances flavor), ½ tsp cardamom powder or 1 cinnamon stick (optional for warm aroma)
ingredients: Step 1: Peel, deseed, and chop 1 kg papaya into small pieces. Mash roughly with a fork or potato masher (helps release juices faster). Step 2: In a heavy-bottom pan or wide pot, combine chopped papaya, 4–5 tbsp lemon juice, pinch salt, and optional cardamom/cinnamon. Let sit 20–30 mins (releases natural juices—no water needed). Step 3: Place pan on medium heat; bring to boil, stirring occasionally. Reduce to low-medium; simmer 35–45 mins, stirring every 4–5 mins (scrape sides/bottom to prevent sticking). Step 4: Gradually add 600–800g sugar (in 2–3 batches); stir until fully dissolved. Mixture will become syrupy & bubble. Continue simmering 10–15 mins more until thick (jam coats spoon & falls slowly in ribbons). Step 5: Test doneness: drop small amount on cold plate—should wrinkle when pushed with finger (if not, cook 5 mins more). Remove cinnamon stick if used. Step 6: Cool completely (thickens more on cooling). Transfer to sterilized glass jar. Cool fully; seal. Refrigerate up to 3–4 weeks. Serve as spread on bread, paratha, or with yogurt. Tip: No pectin—long simmer + lemon sets jam naturally.
directions: 4.7/Home-style
cuisine_path: 50–60 Calories per tbsp, Sugar 10–12g, Vitamin C high
nutrition: Simmer: 40–50 mins
timing: Papaya rich in vitamin C (immunity), vitamin A (skin/eye health), and digestive enzymes (papain—helps protein digestion). Low fat; no added pectin—natural. High sugar—reduce to 500g or use jaggery (adds iron/minerals). Healthier homemade jam—great breakfast spread or snack. Portion 1–2 tbsp; pair with whole-grain bread or yogurt for fiber/protein balance.
health_recommendation: High natural sugars—limit for diabetics/weight control; rich in vitamin C/A from papaya (digestive aid); low fat, no preservatives; moderate portions on toast or yogurt.

ingredients: 4–5 large ripe peaches (about 700–800g—peeled, pitted & chopped), ¾–1 cup granulated sugar (150–200g—adjust to peach sweetness), 2 cups heavy whipping cream (chilled—for richness), 1 can (400g) sweetened condensed milk (for creaminess & sweetness), 2 tbsp fresh lemon juice (for brightness & to prevent browning), 1 tsp vanilla extract, pinch salt (enhances flavor)
directions: Step 1: Peel, pit & chop peaches. Toss with 2 tbsp lemon juice immediately (prevents oxidation). Step 2: Blend ¾ of peaches with ¾–1 cup sugar, condensed milk, vanilla, pinch salt to smooth puree. Chop remaining peaches into small chunks (for texture). Step 3: In chilled bowl, whip 2 cups heavy cream to stiff peaks (3–4 mins with electric beater—don’t overwhip). Step 4: Gently fold peach puree into whipped cream in 3 additions (spatula—retain air for light texture). Fold in chopped peach chunks last. Step 5: Pour into freezer-safe container; cover with plastic wrap (touching surface to prevent ice crystals). Freeze 6–8 hrs or overnight. Step 6: Let soften 5–10 mins before scooping. Serve plain or with extra peach slices. Tip: No eggs/no churn—whipped cream gives light, creamy texture. Chill mixture 2–4 hrs before freezing for smoother result.
nutrition: 320–360 Calories, 22–25g Fat (62–70%), Saturated Fat 14–16g (70–80%), Sugar 30–35g, Protein 5–6g (10%)
health_recommendation: High sugar/fat from condensed milk/cream—treat for special occasions; no eggs make it safer/easier; vitamin C from peaches but calorie-dense; portion control for weight/diabetes; lighter with low-fat alternatives possible.

ingredients: 1 kg ripe peaches (peeled, pitted & chopped—use sweet, soft peaches), 600–800g granulated sugar (60–80% peach weight—start lower), 4–5 tbsp fresh lemon juice (for tartness & natural setting), pinch salt, ½ tsp cinnamon powder or 1 cinnamon stick (optional for warmth), ½ tsp grated ginger (optional for zing)
directions: Step 1: Peel/pit/chop peaches (blanch 30 secs in boiling water then ice bath for easy peeling). Mash lightly. Step 2: In heavy pan, combine peaches, 4–5 tbsp lemon juice, pinch salt, optional cinnamon/ginger. Let sit 20–30 mins (releases juices). Step 3: Bring to boil on medium; reduce low-medium; simmer 35–45 mins, stirring every 4–5 mins (scrape sides/bottom). Step 4: Add sugar in 2–3 batches; stir until dissolves. Continue simmering 10–15 mins until thick (coats spoon, falls slowly). Step 5: Test: drop on cold plate—should wrinkle when pushed. Remove cinnamon stick if used. Step 6: Cool completely; transfer to sterilized jar. Refrigerate up to 3–4 weeks. Tip: No pectin—long simmer + lemon sets jam.
nutrition: 50–60 Calories per tbsp, Sugar 10–12g, Vitamin C high
health_recommendation: Natural fruit sugars high—moderate for diabetics/weight; antioxidants from peaches; low fat; good source of vitamin C; enjoy sparingly as spread.

ingredients: 6–8 large ripe peaches (peeled & pitted—about 1.5 kg), ½–¾ cup sugar or honey (adjust to taste—ripe peaches are naturally sweet), 2–3 tbsp fresh lemon juice (for brightness), 2–3 cups cold water (adjust for thickness), pinch salt (enhances flavor), ice cubes & mint leaves for serving
directions: Step 1: Peel/pit/chop peaches (blanch 30 secs in boiling water then ice bath for easy peeling). Step 2: Blend peaches with sugar/honey, lemon juice, pinch salt, and 1 cup water until smooth. Step 3: Strain through fine sieve or muslin cloth (optional—for clearer juice). Step 4: Add 1–2 cups cold water to adjust consistency (thick or thin as preferred). Step 5: Taste & adjust sweetness/tartness. Chill 1–2 hrs or serve immediately over ice with mint. Step 6: Stir before serving (separates). Tip: Use very ripe peaches—no need for extra sugar.
nutrition: 120–150 Calories per glass, Sugar 20–25g, Vitamin C high
health_recommendation: Refreshing, hydrating; high natural vitamins A/C from peaches; low calorie if no added sugar; good for digestion; limit if watching natural sugars.

ingredients: 1 kg ripe pears (peeled, cored & chopped—use soft, sweet variety), 600–800g granulated sugar (60–80% pear weight), 4–5 tbsp fresh lemon juice, pinch salt, 1 cinnamon stick or ½ tsp cinnamon powder (for warmth), ½ tsp grated ginger (optional)
directions: Step 1: Peel/core/chop pears; toss with 2 tbsp lemon juice. Step 2: In pan, combine pears, remaining lemon juice, salt, cinnamon/ginger. Sit 20–30 mins. Step 3: Boil medium; reduce low-medium; simmer 35–45 mins, stirring often (mash for smooth). Step 4: Add sugar in batches; cook 10–15 mins until thick. Step 5: Test on cold plate. Cool; jar. Refrigerate 3–4 weeks. Tip: No pectin—long simmer sets.
nutrition: 50–60 Calories per tbsp
health_recommendation: High sugars—limit for blood sugar/weight; fiber from pears aids digestion; low fat; comforting spread but moderate intake.

ingredients: 1 kg ripe plums (washed, halved, pitted—use sweet black/red plums), ½–¾ cup sugar or honey (adjust), 4–5 cups water, 2–3 tbsp lemon juice (for brightness), pinch salt, ice & mint for serving
directions: Step 1: Halve/pit plums; chop roughly. Step 2: In pot, add plums, sugar/honey, water; boil 10–15 mins until soft. Step 3: Mash plums; simmer 5 mins more. Step 4: Cool slightly; blend smooth. Step 5: Strain (optional—for clear juice); add lemon juice & salt. Step 6: Chill 1–2 hrs; serve over ice with mint. Tip: Use ripe plums—no extra sugar needed.
nutrition: 100–130 Calories per glass, Sugar 15–20g
health_recommendation: Antioxidant-rich from plums (good for digestion/skin); natural sugars but low calorie; hydrating; avoid excess if diabetic.

ingredients: 1 kg ripe plums (pitted & chopped), ¾–1 cup granulated sugar, 2 cups heavy whipping cream (chilled), 1 can (400g) sweetened condensed milk, 2–3 tbsp fresh lemon juice, 1 tsp vanilla extract, pinch salt
directions: Step 1: Pit/chop plums; toss with 2 tbsp lemon juice. Step 2: Blend plums with sugar, condensed milk, vanilla, salt to smooth puree. Step 3: Whip cream to stiff peaks. Step 4: Fold plum puree into whipped cream gently. Step 5: Freeze in container 6–8 hrs (stir every 30 mins first 2 hrs for no-churn). Step 6: Soften 5–10 mins before serving. Tip: No eggs—whipped cream lightens.
nutrition: 320–360 Calories, 22–25g Fat, Sugar 30–35g
health_recommendation: Creamy treat high in sugar/fat—occasional indulgence; plums add antioxidants/vitamin C; no eggs for simplicity; watch portions for weight/heart health.

ingredients: 1 kg ripe plums (pitted and chopped, skins on), 700g-800g sugar (adjust for sweetness), 2 tbsp lemon juice
directions: Step 1: Wash, pit, and chop plums (leave skins for natural pectin). Step 2: Mix plums with sugar in a large pot; let sit 30 mins to release juices.
